title: Status warns in ungranted linked worktree; worktree new prints session next-step
action: GH #30: sessions cd'd into a linked worktree WITHOUT a grant silently share main's store (resolveRoots P40 default), so bee status shows main's feature/phase and users think isolation exists when it does not. Fix (1): in the status handler (templates/bee.mjs handleStatus / its lib builder), when resolveRoots reports worktreeResolution 'linked-valid' with storeRoot===mainRoot (ungranted), prepend a loud one-line block to status output (text + json field, e.g. worktree_notice) saying this worktree SHARES the main checkout's store (same feature/phase/claims) and naming the paved road: bee worktree new --feature <slug> from main (or worktree register for an existing tree). Fix (2) GH #31: handleWorktreeNew's success output gains an explicit next-step line: open a new session with cwd=<worktree path> to work the feature there; this session stays on main (merge back later via bee worktree merge --id <id>). Tests: extend scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs (status-notice row with a fabricated ungranted linked worktree fixture; worktree-new output contains the next-step line). Self-onboard + render; full verify green.

truths: bee status inside an ungranted linked worktree prints the shares-main-store notice (text and --json) and names both remedies; granted worktrees and ordinary checkouts print nothing new.
bee worktree new success output contains the open-a-session next-step naming the exact path and the merge-back command.
Tests cover both; full run_verify.mjs green; mirrors byte-identical.
prohibitions: Do NOT change resolveRoots/store selection or grant semantics — messaging only.
Do NOT touch hooks/ files (a concurrent cell owns the guard message).

trace.outcome: bee status warns loudly in an ungranted linked worktree (shares main's store, names both remedies); bee worktree new success output gains an explicit next-step naming the created path and the merge-back command. Messaging only.

  "tests_inspected": "scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs full run (real temp git repo + real bee dispatcher via spawnSync, no mocking) covering the register/list/unregister/new/merge lifecycle, including ordinary checkout, ungranted linked worktree (resolveRoots P40 default), and granted linked worktree fixtures already present in the script.",
  "tests_added_or_changed": "4 new rows: (1) status --json/text from the ordinary (main) checkout asserts NO worktree_notice field / no UNGRANTED text; (2) status --json/text from the UNGRANTED wt fixture (already proven ungranted earlier in the script, post explicit --id unregister) asserts worktree_notice present naming both remedies (bee worktree new --feature and bee worktree register); (3) re-registering the SAME wt then asserting the notice is absent (grant-state-driven, not path-driven), then unregistering back; (4) worktree new --json next_step field + a text-output row for a second worktree, asserting the exact path, open-a-new-session wording, stays-on-main wording, and the bee worktree merge --id command.",
  "red_failure_evidence": "Pre-change, buildStatus/renderStatusText had no concept of an ungranted-linked-worktree notice, and handleWorktreeNew's success payload had no next_step field (only a bare Open your next session in <path> line with no session-boundary or merge-back wording). First run of the 4 new rows against the pre-change dispatcher failed exactly as expected: worktree_notice was undefined in JSON (no such key at all), /UNGRANTED/ never matched the plain status text, next_step was undefined in the worktree-new JSON, and /open a new session/i plus /stays on main/ never matched the old single next-step line. All 4 rows failed; the other 123 pre-existing rows in that same run stayed green, proving the test additions themselves introduced no regression before the source change landed.",

Result: 127/127 rows passed (123 pre-existing + 4 new), including explicit absence checks for the ordinary checkout and the granted-worktree case.",
